title: Everyone Is Special
lesson: Everyone looks different and likes different things, and those differences make us special friends.
prompts: In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits cross-legged in a small circle, chatting and gesturing with one hand, with a friendly smile. Around them sit {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN}, chatting together. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits, leaning forward with hands on their knees, with a curious, interested expression, watching a friend share. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} holds up a favourite picture book while {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN} leans in to look. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits proudly holding up their own favourite toy, with a happy, sharing smile. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} clap and look interested. Exactly three children in the frame and no one else. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — four friends, and only four children in total, no extra or duplicate child, exactly four not five, stand in a single row noticing how different they all look. From left to right: first the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image, laughing and gesturing playfully, with a warm smile; then {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, who is the only child wearing red dungarees; then {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN}; then {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN}. Each is one single distinct child, each looking different, laughing and waving. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands holding hands in a circle with friends, with a joyful smile. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} and {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} hold hands too, all together. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, golden light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands in a happy group hug with friends, with a big warm smile. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN} hug in close, all delighted. Exactly four children in the frame and no one else. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.
gists: In a bright classroom on a colourful alphabet rug, the child sits cross-legged in a small circle, chatting and gesturing, while three other children sit chatting together.
On the classroom rug, the child leans forward with hands on knees, curious, watching a friend share; a girl holds up a favourite picture book while a boy leans in to look.
On the classroom rug, the child proudly holds up their own favourite toy with a sharing smile while two friends clap, interested — exactly three children.
On the classroom rug, four children stand in a single row noticing how different they all look — the child laughing and gesturing, then the three friends — exactly four, no duplicates.
On the classroom rug, the child holds hands in a circle with three friends, all joyful — different and special, side by side.
On the classroom rug in golden light, the child is in a happy group hug with three friends, all delighted — exactly four children. Everyone is special.
texts: You sat in a circle with your friends on the colourful classroom rug. Everyone was chatting happily.
One friend held up her favourite picture book to share. You watched, curious and interested.
Then you showed your favourite toy. "This is mine!" you said proudly. Your friends clapped.
You all looked so different from each other — and that made you laugh and smile together.
You held hands in one big circle, all of you together. Different and special, side by side.
Then came a great big group hug! Everyone is special, and that makes the best friends.
